title: What must ship before Grill Visuals replaces Sideshow?
summary: Replacement requires visual quality, complete diagram coverage, a safe local-first trust boundary, and dependable handoff—not merely prettier architecture maps.

options.label: Complete the local slice
options.description: Finishes all seven native families, strict contracts, and visual quality before adding public infrastructure.

options.label: Ship architecture only
options.description: Delivers the strongest canvas early, but cannot replace Sideshow across real grilling questions.

options.label: Build sharing first
options.description: Creates teammate links sooner while leaving diagram coverage and safety gaps unresolved.

root.label: Replacement ready
root.description: Safe default visual system for every Grill Me session

children.label: Visual quality
children.description: Polished enough to aid thinking, not decorate it

children.label: Fluid canvas
children.description: Readable fit, pan, zoom, tracing, and restrained motion

children.label: Family identity
children.description: Each diagram uses its native visual grammar

children.label: Diagram coverage
children.description: Every common grilling shape has a strict renderer

children.label: Seven families
children.description: Architecture through comparison all work locally

children.label: Gap driven
children.description: Visuals appear only when they materially clarify a question

children.label: Trust boundary
children.description: No secret or executable input reaches generated pages

children.label: Strict JSON
children.description: Unknown fields and malformed references are rejected

children.label: Local first
children.description: Public sharing always requires explicit intent

children.label: Session continuity
children.description: Another developer can understand and resume the remaining gaps

children.label: Handoff packet
children.description: PR, decision log, unresolved questions, and visuals travel together

children.label: Clean shutdown
children.description: Temporary visual servers stop when the session ends
